title: [lichen_link_rx.c:282-290] SCHC decompression error codes conflated
description:
Gap: SCHC decompression errors are mapped to generic error codes, losing diagnostic information.

What: Lines 282-290 check the return from lichen_schc_decompress. SCHC_ERR_BUFFER_TOO_SMALL maps to -ENOMEM, but all other errors map to -EINVAL. This loses the specific SCHC error code (malformed rule ID, truncated data, unsupported rule, etc.).

Why it matters: Debugging SCHC decompression failures requires knowing the specific failure mode. Mapping all non-buffer errors to -EINVAL makes it impossible to distinguish between 'invalid rule ID' and 'corrupted compressed data' without code inspection. The original SCHC error code should be logged or a more specific error returned.
close_reason: By design: SCHC errors after MIC verification indicate either corrupted compressed data or buffer sizing issues. At this point, frame authenticity is already verified so detailed error differentiation adds logging overhead without actionable recovery - the frame is dropped regardless. The -EINVAL/-ENOMEM distinction is sufficient for resource vs format diagnosis.
